ELPAC Level 1
1300–1400L
DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS9-L1-13001400-HSELA910-CSR-LAT-01  |  Deck B

The Panel That Carries It

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once.
Listen to the conversation.
1What is the girl mainly explaining to the boy?
2Why does the boy think the girl should add a panel earlier in the comic?
3Based on the conversation, what does the girl realize about her storyboard?

Answer all three questions. You may change your answers before submitting.

Answer all 3 questions to submit.
Go back and listen to the girl's first turn. Did she say anything about changing which story they're using? What did she say she was doing?
Listen again to what the girl says about the abuela. Is she describing how the character learned something, or what the character is showing the abuela?
Was there a teacher in the conversation? Listen again to the boy's turn and see if he mentions what a teacher said.
The boy does mention the abuela appears at the end — but is that his reason for the earlier panel? What does he say the earlier panel should show?
Listen to the girl's response to the boy. Does she say her original panel choice was wrong? Or does she say something different about it?
Did either speaker say anything about the reader having trouble understanding the theme? What does the girl say the earlier panel does for the third panel?
